description: The study of shapes, sizes, positions, and properties of figures in space — from Euclidean plane geometry through coordinate geometry, differential geometry, and modern topology-adjacent topics.

concepts.name: Point
concepts.description: A dimensionless location in space with no length, width, or height; the fundamental primitive of Euclidean geometry.

concepts.name: Line
concepts.description: A one-dimensional figure extending infinitely in both directions, determined by any two distinct points.

concepts.name: Line Segment
concepts.description: The part of a line between two endpoints; has a finite measurable length.

concepts.name: Ray
concepts.description: A part of a line with one endpoint that extends infinitely in one direction.

concepts.name: Plane
concepts.description: A two-dimensional flat surface extending infinitely; determined by any three non-collinear points.

concepts.name: Angle
concepts.description: The figure formed by two rays sharing a common endpoint (vertex); measured in degrees or radians.

concepts.name: Types of Angles
aliases: acute, obtuse, right, straight, reflex angles
concepts.description: Classification of angles: acute (0°–90°), right (90°), obtuse (90°–180°), straight (180°), reflex (180°–360°).

concepts.name: Angle Measurement
concepts.description: Angles are measured in degrees (1 full rotation = 360°) or radians (1 full rotation = 2π); a protractor measures angles physically.

concepts.name: Angle Pairs
concepts.description: Special pairs of angles: complementary (sum 90°), supplementary (sum 180°), vertical angles (equal across an intersection), and linear pairs (supplementary adjacent).

concepts.name: Parallel Lines
aliases: parallel lines cut by a transversal
concepts.description: Lines that never meet; when cut by a transversal they produce equal corresponding angles, alternate interior angles, and co-interior supplementary angles.

concepts.name: Perpendicular Lines
concepts.description: Two lines intersecting at 90°; the perpendicular from a point to a line is the shortest segment from the point to the line.

concepts.name: Triangle
concepts.description: A polygon with three sides and three angles whose sum is 180°; the simplest non-trivial polygon and the basis of trigonometry.

concepts.name: Types of Triangles
aliases: scalene, isosceles, equilateral, acute, obtuse, right triangle
concepts.description: Classification by sides: scalene (all sides different), isosceles (two sides equal), equilateral (all sides equal). By angles: acute, right, obtuse.

concepts.name: Triangle Angle Sum Theorem
concepts.description: The sum of the three interior angles of any triangle in Euclidean geometry equals 180°.

concepts.name: Right Triangle
concepts.description: A triangle with one 90° angle; the side opposite the right angle (hypotenuse) is the longest side and satisfies the Pythagorean theorem.

concepts.name: Pythagorean Theorem
concepts.description: In a right triangle with legs a, b and hypotenuse c: a² + b² = c². One of the most fundamental results in all of mathematics with hundreds of proofs.

concepts.name: Converse of the Pythagorean Theorem
concepts.description: If a² + b² = c² for sides a, b, c of a triangle, then the triangle is right-angled with hypotenuse c.

concepts.name: Congruent Triangles
concepts.description: Two triangles are congruent if they have the same shape and size; established by the congruence criteria SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, and HL (for right triangles).

concepts.name: Similar Triangles
concepts.description: Two triangles are similar if they have the same shape (proportional sides and equal angles); established by AA, SAS, or SSS similarity criteria.

concepts.name: Triangle Centers
concepts.description: Special points associated with a triangle: centroid (intersection of medians), circumcenter (circumscribed circle center), incenter (inscribed circle center), orthocenter (altitude intersection).

concepts.name: Area of a Triangle
concepts.description: Area = ½ × base × height; also Heron's formula: √(s(s−a)(s−b)(s−c)) where s is the semiperimeter.

concepts.name: Polygon
concepts.description: A closed plane figure bounded by straight line segments; characterized by the number of sides and classified as regular (all sides and angles equal) or irregular.

concepts.name: Polygon Angle Sum
concepts.description: The sum of interior angles of a convex n-gon is (n−2)×180°; each interior angle of a regular n-gon is (n−2)×180°/n.

concepts.name: Quadrilateral
concepts.description: A polygon with four sides; includes parallelograms, rectangles, rhombuses, squares, trapezoids, and kites, classified by side and angle properties.

concepts.name: Parallelogram
concepts.description: A quadrilateral with both pairs of opposite sides parallel; includes rectangles (90° angles), rhombuses (all sides equal), and squares (both).

concepts.name: Trapezoid
concepts.description: A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides (the bases); area = ½(b₁ + b₂)h.

concepts.name: Regular Polygon
concepts.description: A polygon with all sides equal and all angles equal; inscribed in a circle, approaching the circle as n → ∞.

concepts.name: Area of Polygons
concepts.description: Computing areas of polygons by decomposition into triangles or by standard formulas (parallelogram: bh; trapezoid: ½(b₁+b₂)h; regular polygon: ½ap).

concepts.name: Area
concepts.description: The measure of the two-dimensional extent of a figure; for curved regions computed via integration.

concepts.name: Perimeter
concepts.description: The total length of the boundary of a two-dimensional figure; for circles it is called the circumference.

concepts.name: Circle
concepts.description: The set of all points in a plane equidistant from a fixed center point; the fundamental round figure of 2D geometry.

concepts.name: Parts of a Circle
concepts.description: Radius (center to boundary), diameter (chord through center), chord (segment joining two boundary points), arc (portion of boundary), sector (pie slice), tangent (line touching at one point).

concepts.name: Circumference of a Circle
concepts.description: The perimeter of a circle: C = 2πr = πd, where r is the radius and π ≈ 3.14159…

concepts.name: Area of a Circle
concepts.description: A = πr²; derivable via integration or as the limit of inscribed regular polygon areas.

concepts.name: Circle Theorems
concepts.description: Key theorems: inscribed angle = half central angle; tangent perpendicular to radius; equal chords equidistant from center; power of a point.

concepts.name: Equation of a Circle
concepts.description: The equation of a circle with center (h, k) and radius r is (x−h)² + (y−k)² = r²; derivable from the distance formula.

concepts.name: Geometric Proof
concepts.description: A rigorous deductive argument establishing a geometric truth from axioms, definitions, and previously proved theorems; typically presented in two-column or paragraph form.

concepts.name: Length
concepts.description: The one-dimensional measure of a line segment; extended to curves via arc length.

concepts.name: Surface Area
concepts.description: The total area of all faces of a three-dimensional solid; for curved surfaces computed via surface integrals.

concepts.name: Volume
concepts.description: The three-dimensional measure of the space enclosed by a solid; computed by formulas for standard solids or by integration.

concepts.name: Three-Dimensional Solids
concepts.description: Geometric solids: prisms (uniform cross-section), pyramids (apex), cones, cylinders, spheres; characterized by faces, edges, vertices and their counts satisfying Euler's formula V−E+F=2.

concepts.name: Platonic Solids
concepts.description: The five convex regular polyhedra (tetrahedron, cube, octahedron, dodecahedron, icosahedron); the only regular polyhedra in 3D, classified by Euclid.

concepts.name: Coordinate Plane
concepts.description: The plane ℝ² with two perpendicular number lines (x-axis, y-axis) intersecting at the origin, enabling algebraic representation of geometric objects.

concepts.name: Cartesian Coordinates
concepts.description: The pair (x, y) locating a point in the coordinate plane by its signed distances from the y-axis (x) and x-axis (y).

concepts.name: Quadrants
concepts.description: The four regions of the coordinate plane divided by the axes: Quadrant I (x>0, y>0), II (x<0, y>0), III (x<0, y<0), IV (x>0, y<0).

concepts.name: Distance Formula
concepts.description: The distance between (x₁, y₁) and (x₂, y₂) is √((x₂−x₁)² + (y₂−y₁)²); a direct application of the Pythagorean theorem in coordinates.

concepts.name: Midpoint Formula
concepts.description: The midpoint of a segment from (x₁, y₁) to (x₂, y₂) is ((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2).

concepts.name: Slope
concepts.description: The slope of a line is the ratio of vertical change to horizontal change between any two points; m = (y₂−y₁)/(x₂−x₁); equal for parallel lines, negative reciprocals for perpendicular.

concepts.name: Equations of Lines
concepts.description: y = mx + b (slope-intercept), y − y₁ = m(x − x₁) (point-slope), ax + by = c (standard form); three equivalent representations of a line.

concepts.name: Conic Sections
concepts.description: Curves formed by the intersection of a double cone and a plane: circles, ellipses, parabolas, and hyperbolas; unified by the general second-degree equation.

concepts.name: Parabola
concepts.description: A conic section equidistant from a focus and a directrix; graph of a quadratic function; opens up/down (y = ax²+bx+c) or left/right (x = ay²+by+c).

concepts.name: Ellipse
concepts.description: A conic section where the sum of distances from two foci is constant; equation x²/a² + y²/b² = 1; a circle is a special case with equal foci.

concepts.name: Hyperbola
concepts.description: A conic section where the difference of distances from two foci is constant; has two branches and asymptotes y = ±(b/a)x.

concepts.name: Polar Coordinates
concepts.description: An alternative 2D coordinate system using distance r from the origin and angle θ from the positive x-axis; related to Cartesian by x = r cos θ, y = r sin θ.

concepts.name: Polar Curves
concepts.description: Curves expressed in polar form r = f(θ); includes cardioids, roses, lemniscates, and Archimedean spirals.

concepts.name: Geometric Transformations
concepts.description: Functions mapping the plane to itself that preserve shape properties; isometries (translations, rotations, reflections) preserve distance; dilations scale by a factor.

concepts.name: Translation
concepts.description: A transformation that moves every point by the same vector (h, k), preserving shape, size, and orientation.

concepts.name: Rotation
aliases: rotation by θ about a point
concepts.description: A transformation that turns every point by angle θ about a fixed center; expressed by a 2×2 rotation matrix.

concepts.name: Reflection
concepts.description: A transformation that flips every point over a line of reflection, reversing orientation.

concepts.name: Dilation
concepts.description: A transformation scaling distances from a center by a constant factor k; produces a similar figure of different size.

concepts.name: Vectors in 2D
concepts.description: A quantity with both magnitude and direction in the plane, represented by an ordered pair; added head-to-tail and scaled by multiplication.

concepts.name: Vectors in 3D
concepts.description: Vectors in three-dimensional space; introduced by extending the 2D framework to triples (a, b, c) with dot and cross products.

concepts.name: Dot Product
concepts.description: The dot product a·b = Σaᵢbᵢ = |a||b|cos θ; measures the component of one vector in the direction of another; zero iff orthogonal.

concepts.name: Cross Product
concepts.description: The cross product a × b is a vector perpendicular to both a and b with magnitude |a||b|sin θ; defined only in ℝ³ (and ℝ⁷).

concepts.name: Curves in Space
concepts.description: Smooth curves in ℝⁿ studied via their parametrization; local geometry described by curvature κ and torsion τ via the Frenet-Serret apparatus.
references: do Carmo 1976 Differential Geometry of Curves and Surfaces

concepts.name: Curvature
concepts.description: The rate at which a curve bends; κ = |dT/ds| where T is the unit tangent vector and s is arc length.

concepts.name: Frenet-Serret Formulas
concepts.description: The system T' = κN, N' = −κT + τB, B' = −τN relating the tangent T, normal N, binormal B and encoding all local geometry of a space curve.

concepts.name: Differential Geometry of Surfaces
concepts.description: The study of smooth surfaces in ℝ³ via first and second fundamental forms, Gaussian curvature K, mean curvature H, and the Gauss-Bonnet theorem.
references: Spivak 1979 A Comprehensive Introduction to Differential Geometry

concepts.name: Geometric Constructions
concepts.description: Constructions achievable using only compass and straightedge; includes bisecting angles and segments, constructing regular polygons; Galois theory classifies which are possible.
